answer:
In Charles Comte's *Traité de la propriété*, Volume I, Chapter 4 ("De l'occupation des choses"), his prose slips toward Latin when describing Roman jurists' views on occupation: "Les jurisconsultes romains admettent que tout animal sauvage devient la propriété de celui qui s'en empare le premier [...]. Enfin, ils déclarent que **toute île qui se forme au milieu de la mer devient la propriété du premier qui s'en met en possession : *nullius enim esse creditur***."[^1]

The quoted Latin maxim (*nullius enim esse creditur*, "for it is considered [to belong] to no one")—drawn from Justinian's *Institutes* and *Digest*—authorizes classifying newly emergent islands (along with wild animals, pearls on the seashore, etc.) as ownerless (*res nullius*), naturally conceding them to the first occupant without further proof or process.[^2] A purely French philosophical argument would instead need to establish *why* such things lack any prior owner (no natural title from creation or prior use), deriving the occupier's right from first principles of liberty and utility; the Latin shorthand invokes Roman juridical tradition as self-evident authorization.[^3]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL I*, [Chapter 4, paragraph p-02fc65](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/04-de-loccupation-des-choses/original/#p-02fc65).

[^2]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL I*, [Chapter 4, note 11](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/04-de-loccupation-des-choses/original/#p-e63e73) (citing Gaius and Justinian); cf. the fuller maxim in note 6: "*Quod enim nullius est, id ratione naturali occupanti conceditur*."

[^3]: The same chapter opens with another near-untranslated juridical term and maxim: "*res nullius*" and "*Quod enim nullius est...*," treated as axiomatic.[^1] A parallel instance of "*res nullius*" (untranslated) appears in Volume I, Chapter 22.[^4]

[^4]: Charles Comte, *Traité de la propriété: VOL I*, [Chapter 22, paragraph p-aa668c](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-la-propriete-vol-i-2c7a99/22-de-la-propriete-des-richesses-minerales-et-des-lim/original/#p-aa668c).
